text.title: Docker Production Checklists and Quick Audit Commands
text.summary: Dockerfile quality, security, production readiness, Compose, multi-stage build, and CI/CD integration checklists; quick audit commands.

list.item: Non-root user created and set with USER instruction
No secrets in image (no ENV for passwords, no ARG secrets baked into layers)
Base image pinned to specific version (not latest)
Base image scanned for CVEs (Trivy or Docker Scout)
HEALTHCHECK configured with appropriate interval and timeout
Resource limits set (memory and CPU) at runtime
.dockerignore present and excludes: .git, node_modules, .env, test files, docs
Layer caching optimized: dependency files copied before source code
RUN commands combined where appropriate to reduce layers
Multi-stage build used when build-time tools are needed
WORKDIR set before any COPY or RUN
COPY used instead of ADD unless extracting archives
Exec form used for CMD and ENTRYPOINT
EXPOSE documents ports even if not enforced
Cache mounts used for package managers: --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/pip
No private keys or certificates in image layers
Read-only root filesystem enabled at runtime (--read-only) where possible
All capabilities dropped (--cap-drop ALL) with only required caps added back
no-new-privileges security option set
Internal-only services not exposed on host ports
Docker Secrets used for sensitive runtime values (not ENV)
Regular image scanning in CI/CD; critical CVEs block deployment
Image tagged with semantic version + git SHA (not just latest)
Image size optimized (below 500MB for most applications)
Logging to stdout/stderr in JSON structured format
SIGTERM handled gracefully (pre-stop hook or signal handler in app)
All configuration via environment variables with sensible defaults
Restart policy defined (unless-stopped or always)
Rolling update strategy defined

example.code:
# Scan for CVEs trivy image myapp:latest docker scout cves myapp:latest
# Lint Dockerfile hadolint Dockerfile
# Analyze layer sizes dive myapp:latest docker history myapp:latest
# Check running container security posture docker inspect --format='{{.Config.User}}' container_name docker inspect --format='{{.HostConfig.ReadonlyRootfs}}' container_name docker inspect --format='{{.HostConfig.CapDrop}}' container_name
# Resource usage docker stats container_name
# Health status docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' container_name

text.reference: Compose template with health checks and named networks
